episodes.title: Is Angi Worth It? The Real Cost of a Shared Lead
episodes.summary: The honest math on Angi, Thumbtack, and HomeAdvisor — why your leads get shared with up to 12 pros, what it really costs per booked job, and what to do instead. Full breakdown + free calculator at booked-job.com.
episodes.script: Welcome to Booked Job, the show for service pros who'd rather be working. Today, one question every contractor eventually asks: is Angi actually worth it? Let's do the honest math. An Angi lead looks cheap. Fifteen, thirty-five, maybe a hundred bucks. But that's not the number that matters. The number that matters is your cost per booked job. Here's why. Angi, Thumbtack, and HomeAdvisor all run the same model: they sell that one lead to as many as five to twelve contractors. So the second you buy it, you're racing four, six, ten other trucks to the same homeowner's phone. Close rates on shared leads are low, often ten to twenty-five percent. So once you count all the leads that didn't close, your real cost per won job lands somewhere around fourteen hundred to twenty-five hundred dollars. Compare that to about two hundred and ninety dollars for a job you win through your own website or a referral. But the cost isn't even the worst part. The worst part is what happens when you stop paying. The leads stop. Instantly. Because you didn't build anything. You rented an audience. Every dollar you put into shared leads is a dollar that didn't go into something you own, something that keeps producing after you turn it off. So is Angi ever worth it? Maybe, if you're brand new, no website, no reviews, no pipeline at all, and you just need at-bats while you build. But for anyone with even a small online presence, that money is better spent on channels you own. Your Google Business Profile. A simple referral system. Identifying the homeowners already visiting your site and following up before they call the next guy. Those don't disappear when you stop paying. They compound. Here's the one-sentence version: Angi isn't a lead source. It's a tax on not having your own. If you want the full breakdown, including a free calculator that shows your real cost per Angi lead based on your own close rate and ticket size, head to booked-job dot com. Thanks for listening to Booked Job. Stay booked.
